zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke於1130年出生在英國英格蘭肯特托布利其時，他的父親Gilbert FitzGilbert de Clare Earl of Pembroke30歲，母親Elizabeth Beaumont28歲。他於1170年8月26日與Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster在愛爾蘭迪 本 度布林結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1176年4月20日在愛爾蘭萊音斯特享年46歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭迪 本 度布林。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke于1130年出生在英国英格兰肯特托布利其时，他的父亲Gilbert FitzGilbert de Clare Earl of Pembroke30岁，母亲Elizabeth Beaumont28岁。他于1170年8月26日与Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster在爱尔兰迪 本 度布林结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1176年4月20日在爱尔兰萊音斯特享年46岁，埋葬在爱尔兰迪 本 度布林。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

historicEvents.description: Arrival of Normans at Baginbun in Co. Wexford thus started 800 year struggle between English and Irish.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke was born in 1130, in Tonbridge, Kent, England, United Kingdom, his father, Gilbert FitzGilbert de Clare Earl of Pembroke, was 30 and his mother, Elizabeth Beaumont, was 28. He married Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster on 26 August 1170, in Diocese of Dublin, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died on 20 April 1176, in Leinster, Ireland, at the age of 46, and was buried in Diocese of Dublin, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke est né en 1130, à Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gilbert FitzGilbert de Clare Earl of Pembroke, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Beaumont, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster le 26 août 1170, à Diocese of Dublin, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 20 avril 1176, à Leinster, Irlande, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Diocese of Dublin, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke 1130 in Tonbridge, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gilbert FitzGilbert de Clare Earl of Pembroke 30 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Beaumont 28. Er hat am 26. August 1170 in Diocese of Dublin, Irland Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 20. April 1176 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Leinster, Irland und wurde in Diocese of Dublin, Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke è nato il nel 1130 nel Tonbridge, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Gilbert FitzGilbert de Clare Earl of Pembroke, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Beaumont, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster il 26 agosto 1170, nel Diocese of Dublin, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 20 aprile 1176, nel Leinster, Irlanda, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolto a Diocese of Dublin, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembrokeが1130年にイギリスイギリスケントトンブリッジで生まれた時，父Gilbert FitzGilbert de Clare Earl of Pembrokeは30歳，母Elizabeth Beaumontは28歳であった。1170年8月26日にアイルランドディオセゼ オフ ダブリンでPrincess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinsterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1176年4月20日にアイルランドレインスターで46歳で死去し，アイルランドディオセゼ オフ ダブリンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke(이)가 1130년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 토브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gilbert FitzGilbert de Clare Earl of Pembroke(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Beaumont(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1170년 8월 26일 아일랜드 디오스스 프 드블린에서 Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1176년 4월 20일 아일랜드 레인스터에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 디오스스 프 드블린에 묻혔다.

pt.Tintern Abbey, Tintern, Monmouthshire, W: Tintern Abbey, Tintern, Monmouthshire, Gales, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke nasceu em 1130, em Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Gilbert FitzGilbert de Clare Earl of Pembroke, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Beaumont, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster em 26 de agosto de 1170, em Diocese of Dublin, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 20 de abril de 1176, em Leinster, Irlanda, com 46 anos, e foi sepultado em Diocese of Dublin, Irlanda.

ru.Tintern Abbey, Tintern, Monmouthshire, W: тинтирн эбби, тинтирн, монмоутшир, Уэльс, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke родился в 1130 г. в местности тонбридж, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Gilbert FitzGilbert de Clare Earl of Pembroke было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Beaumont, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster 26 августа 1170 г. в местности диосес оф дублинь, Ирландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер 20 апреля 1176 г. в местности линстир, Ирландия в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности диосес оф дублинь, Ирландия.

es.Tintern Abbey, Tintern, Monmouthshire, W: Tintern Abbey, Tintern, Monmouthshire, Gales, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke nació en 1130, en Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Gilbert FitzGilbert de Clare Earl of Pembroke, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Beaumont, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster el 26 de agosto de 1170, en Diocese of Dublin, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 20 de abril de 1176, en Leinster, Irlanda, a la edad de 46 años, y fue sepultado en Diocese of Dublin, Irlanda.
